hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The solver must inspect code, runtime traces, generated trees, and persisted rows to find the divergence.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: The repair spans Go service state, persisted records, C traversal output, shell scenarios, and verifier-derived filesystem observations.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose
hardness_axes.reasoning: The prompt reports restored-tree drift without naming replay order, identity grouping, or metadata propagation.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Changes that repair one scenario can break restart, identity, or accounting invariants elsewhere.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: The task is not a standard hardlink copy or backup script recipe; it requires restart-sensitive filesystem state reasoning.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The instruction can honestly state the audit outcome without revealing the internal coupling.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Multiple generated profiles are checked, not a single secret corrupt artifact.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The planned fix requires coordinated code changes across several subsystem roots.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Tests cover several profile combinations and require behavior that generalizes.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A complete public contract still leaves the solver to diagnose why the service drifts.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty rests on system reasoning, not merely on anti-tamper measures.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The idea is semantic and not a verifier dependency or timeout task.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The flipping contract distributes failures so no small obvious cluster controls the suite.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The oracle is planned as multi-file coordinated logic, not a whole-file replacement of one helper.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The task cannot be solved by editing one table, manifest, or config block.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction nouns are banned from fix-path symbols and planned test names are neutral.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fix-path function names are opaque and decoys structurally rhyme with them.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A standard hardlink-preserving copy recipe is insufficient because restart and accounting interactions are tested.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The task does not rely on security flavor for perceived hardness.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The requirements are coupled: identity, replay, and accounting fixes affect each other.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The shell/C/Go harness provides realism but the claimed hardness is in the service semantics.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A frontier model should need experiments and code tracing beyond opening obvious files.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The combined failure mode is clearly hard system debugging rather than medium implementation work.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Four non-trivial discoveries are enumerated with planned locations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The planned instruction is symptoms-only and avoids cause disclosure.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three topologies each require at least three coordinated locations.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Deterministic generated workspaces and independent inspections make results programmatically checkable.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: The output path and equivalence contract can be stated concisely without overspecifying internals.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: An expert can repair the small Go/C system in a few hours.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: The task demands professional filesystem and service-state debugging.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Snapshot/restore drift is operationally valuable to diagnose.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Tests grade the resulting trees, records, and report rather than implementation choices.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Reading only instruction.md will not reveal how persisted rows, object grouping, accounting, and C observations interact.

discovery_budget.discovery: Persisted operations are not replayed in the same effective order after a process boundary.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming replay order would make the repair site and invariant obvious.

discovery_budget.discovery: Names and backing file identity are treated inconsistently across capture and materialization.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: It would reduce the task to a standard hardlink-preservation recipe.

discovery_budget.discovery: Accounting attributes move on a different lifecycle than restored file objects.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: It exposes the metadata propagation invariant that should be inferred from behavior.

discovery_budget.discovery: The C traversal helper and Go grouping code disagree on stable observations for generated cases.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: It over-narrows the search to the helper boundary.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: The planned prompt names the bad audit outcome and output path but does not identify causes, algorithms, files, flags, or thresholds.

attack_path.summary: Likely trivialization attempt: patch one replay helper or fake the report.
attack_path.reasoning: The planned tests independently inspect files, records, and scenario workspaces, so a report-only or one-helper patch cannot satisfy all cases.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: Coordinate persisted row folding, object grouping, accounting transfer, and traversal normalization.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: Any smaller patch should fail at least one of the clean, restart, dense, transition, or mixed scenarios.

collapse_audit.reasoning: The proposed construction leaves nontrivial residual work after the instruction and file tree are visible.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: The solver must synthesize runtime behavior across Go state, persisted records, native traversal, and verifier-derived filesystem observations.

topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Ordering, grouping, metadata, and observations must all agree.
Captured graph shape, encoding, and emitted records must be coordinated.
Native observations must be normalized before grouping and accounting can converge.

symbol_table.purpose: merges an incoming item into an in-memory graph used by later materialization
orders persisted rows into a deterministic stream consumed by the caller

symbol_table.signature: func mark_c(a *mapX, b uint64, c []entryX) error
symbol_table.purpose: carries per-period attributes from input entries into emitted records
decides whether two graph nodes should share the same emitted backing object

symbol_table.signature: int walk_e(struct qx *a, const char *b, unsigned long c)
symbol_table.purpose: records stable observations from a filesystem traversal for the Go caller

decoy_manifest.non_fix_purpose: maintains transient CLI display state for non-service commands
summarizes profile statistics for documentation and dry-run output
implements a shallow listing mode used by diagnostics but not the audit path

renames_during_drafting.reason: Avoided restore/link leakage from planned instruction vocabulary.
Removed direct scenario nouns from the test surface.
